title: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet
tagline: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Saidapet Kamakshi Amman Temple is a Hindu temple in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Saidapet Kamakshi Amman Temple is a Hindu temple in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. Located in Chetty Street in the neighbourhood of Saidapet, the temple is administered by the Vishwakarma community. The presiding deity is the goddess Kamakshi. The temple is located near the Saidapet railway station and is believed to be more than 300 years old.

topPlaces.name: Saidapet
topPlaces.description: Saidapet is a neighbourhood of Chennai, India situated about 12.20 kilometres (7.58 miles) from kilometre zero of Chennai city. Historically, an important suburban town of Madras city, Saidapet served as the southern gateway to the city for long by virtue of its strategic location on the Mount Road stretch of the Grand Southern Trunk Road until its inclusion in the limits of the then Corporation of Madras city in 1946.

topPlaces.name: Jafferkhanpet
topPlaces.description: Jafferkhanpet is a locality of Chennai, India. The PIN code of the locality is 600083.

topPlaces.name: China Bazaar Road, Chennai
topPlaces.description: China Bazaar Road, officially Netaji Subash Chandra Bose Road (or NSC Bose Road), is one of the main thoroughfares of the commercial centre of George Town in Chennai, India. The road connects Rajaji Salai in the east and Wall Tax Road in the west.

topPlaces.name: Karaneeswarar Temple, Saidapet
topPlaces.description: Karaneeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the neighborhood of Saidapet in Chennai, India. It is dedicated to Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Little Mount Shrine

topPlaces.name: Vasanth TV
topPlaces.description: Vasanth TV is a Tamil entertainment satellite channel which is owned by Vinoth Vasanthakumar of Vasanth & Co Group. The company has tied up with Tata Communications for providing the uplinking facilities.

topPlaces.name: Little Mount, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Chinnamalai, translated in English as Little Mount, is a hillock that lies on the bank of Adayar River in the Saidapet taluk of Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. Anna Salai, previously called Mount Road, is a long road in Chennai that stretches between Chennai northern side to the southern side at Little Mount.

topPlaces.name: SPIC building, Chennai
topPlaces.description: SPIC building, Chennai, is one of the oldest skyscrapers of the city. Earlier, it was popularly known as "twin towers of Chennai." The main building has 10 floors and Annexe building has 8 floors.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet?
faq.a: Stays in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamakshi Amman Temple, Saidapet?
faq.a: The top attractions are Saidapet, Jafferkhanpet, China Bazaar Road, Chennai, Karaneeswarar Temple, Saidapet, Little Mount Shrine.
